### Post by etement on 2006-04-17
I'm not sure if this is correct, but I tried running Snex9x through Wine and and it said it doens't run in 24-bit, only 16 and 32. 

this is my xorg.conf

```

	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"

	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NV40 [GeForce 6800]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

I've tried changing the 'Default' to 32, but then X refused to start and gave an error. ](*,)  Any help would be appreciated.

---

### Post by localzuk on 2006-04-17
I think this is a bug in wine. 32bit colour in Windows is the same as 24bit in linux.   Take a look at the Wine HQ site [http://www.winehq.org](http://www.winehq.org) for information.

---

### Post by etement on 2006-04-18
thank you. I didn't know that windows 32-bit is the same as linux 24-bit. Why is that?

I'll look into it though

---

### Post by localzuk on 2006-04-18
Most graphics cards cannot support true 32bit colour - so in fact you are only seeing 24bit colour really. Linux provides a 32bit framebuffer which is fed the true 24bit colour IIRC. I couldn't find a definitive answer to this anywhere - it may be worth asking over at X.org.

---

### Post by charnooh on 2006-04-21
There is no "true 32-bit mode". 32-bit mode provided by graphics cards includes alpha channel which defines opacity of the screen and is used for transparent polygons not to cover the other ones (it looks odd). So even in 32-bit mode only 24-bit (16777216) colour are displayed (cause how can you display alpha channel?)

---
